headline: Cursor 3 drops multi-agent chaos, Claude Code source leaks 512k lines, Copilot unlocks new tools

stories.title: Cursor 3 turns developers into AI managers with parallel agents
stories.summary: Cursor dropped Cursor 3 on April 2 with an Agents Window that lets you spin up multiple AI agents working simultaneously across local and cloud environments. You're basically managing a team now, not writing code yourself tbh.
stories.takeaway: The shift from 'autocomplete assistant' to 'full task executor' is complete—Cursor's betting everything on letting you delegate entire features to agents while you oversee.

stories.title: Claude Code source leak exposed 512k lines of agent architecture
stories.summary: Anthropic accidentally shipped a source map in a npm package on March 31 that exposed the entire Claude Code codebase—512k lines, 40 built-in tools, memory systems, orchestration logic. Whoops. The leak showed how Claude Code actually works internally, and lowkey it was pretty sophisticated engineering.
stories.takeaway: The real story wasn't the data breach (there wasn't one) but what the code revealed: fork-join parallelism via KV cache, multi-layer memory design, and a literal Tamagotchi pet feature (/buddy) planned for April 1st.

stories.title: Copilot SDK goes public with agentic runtime you can embed anywhere
stories.summary: GitHub released the Copilot SDK in public preview, letting you embed their production-tested agent runtime directly into your apps. No more building your own orchestration layer—you get tool invocation, streaming, file ops out of the box.
stories.takeaway: GitHub's making Copilot a platform move, not just a product. Developers can now extend and customize agentic capabilities without rebuilding the entire agent stack from scratch.

stories.title: Claude Code adds /powerup lessons and stronger policy controls for teams
stories.summary: Claude Code shipped interactive /powerup lessons showing features with animated demos, plus stricter managed settings for team admins and faster file editing. The Bedrock setup wizard finally makes 3rd-party setup less painful.
stories.takeaway: Anthropic's tightening the onboarding and compliance story—easier for enterprises to control Claude Code behavior while improving accessibility for individual users with guided tutorials.

watchNext.title: Simon Willison's take on 'vibing' vs coding—the philosophical shift
watchNext.why: He literally reframed agentic development as 'vibing now, not coding' and explains why this changes how developers think about their craft. Worth understanding the mindset shift happening.

watchNext.title: Replit Agent 4 went parallel—task queues, simultaneous execution, design-code fusion
watchNext.why: Replit's agent can now run multiple tasks in parallel with full visibility before merging. Also unified design and code environments so you can iterate on UI without breaking the app.
